title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miss Marple and Mrs. McGillicuddy travel by taxi to Rutherford Hall. Miss Marple expresses a sense of duty to investigate potential further killings, despite the police presence. Upon arrival, Miss Marple uses the pretext of needing to use the upstairs bathroom to gain entry to the house.

Inside, Miss Marple introduces herself and Mrs. McGillicuddy to Emma and Cedric. During the visit, Cedric mentions rumors circulating that arsenic was placed in the curry chicken and suggests that Lucy's aunt might have known about it. Miss Marple clarifies that her previous mention of arsenic was merely a guess.

Mr. Creakle-Soper enters and demands tea, complaining about rising taxes and the potential for socialists to seize property. Lucy and Brian arrive with tea and sandwiches to celebrate Dr. Campbell's birthday, an occasion Mr. Creakle-Soper dismisses with disdain. During the gathering, Miss Marple remarks to Brian that he resembles a man she knew in St. Mary Mead named Ronnie Wells, a banker's son who lost his fortune in an East African cargo business. Brian denies being related to him. As Emma comments on the view from the window, Brian begins to explain the science behind the noise of jet planes. Amidst the conversation, Mrs. McGillicuddy whispers to Emma, expressing discomfort with Miss Marple's actions, while Miss Marple asks for permission to use the upstairs bathroom.
